notes: All 15 reps correctly identify the chart type, axis ranges, three series (with correct stations/colors), atmospheric CO2 values, and the acidification trend — the structural baseline is strong. The recurring errors cluster in two areas: (1) misdating the ocean pCO2/pH series start (~1990) to 1983-1994, and (2) inventing final ocean pCO2 values (~400 ppm) above the chart's ~380 ppm. The worst responses (t0.4|rep1, t0.7|rep1, t0.7|rep2) confuse pCO2 units for pH-scale values (e.g., 'pCO2 ~= 8.23' or '~800-1200 μatm') and, in t0.7|rep2, wrongly accuse the figure of a labeling error. Lower temperature (0.1) reps were not reliably more accurate than 0.4/0.7 reps; t0.4|rep2 and t0.7|rep4 were among the best.
